titleStmt.title: SII 11.61: original edition by C. R. Krishnamacharlu, R. S. Panchamukhi, N. Lakshminaraya Rao and H. K. Narasimhaswami
No. 61. (B.K. No. 60 of 1928-29). MARŌḶ, HUNGUND TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT. ON A SLAB SET UP INSIDE THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALIṄGA.

summary.p: The inscription belongs to the reign of Jagadēkamalladēva (I) and registers a gift of land made to a basadi at Maravoḷal (the modern Marōḷ) by the Noḷaṁba chief Ghaṭeyaṅkakāṟa who was governing the Noḷaṁbavāḍi and Kariviḍi-Thirty divisions. Maruvoḷal was under the administration of Mahādēvī, a daughter of Sattiga (i.e., Iṟivabeḍaṅga Satyāśraya). The Jaina teachers Anantavīrya-Munipa who was versed in all the śāstras, Guṇakīrti-Siddhānta-Bhaṭṭāraka and his desciple Dēvakīrti-Paṇḍita are also mentioned. The record is engraved indistinctly in a cursive script.

We know from the Ālūr inscription of Vikramāditya V Śaka 933); Ep. Ind. Vol. XVI, pp. 27 ff.) that Iṟiva-Noḷaṁbādhirāja Ghaṭeyaṅkakāṟa had married a daughter of Iṟivabeḍaṅga. She was probably the Mahādēvī of the present inscription. Iṟiva-Nolaṁbādhirāja Ghaṭeyaṅkakāṟa is apparently identical with Ghaṭeyaṅkakāṟa of the present record.
The record is dated Śaka 946, Raktākshin, Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkramaṇa, the English equivalent of which would be A.D. 1024, December 24. Thursday.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śrīmat kamaḷadēva bhaṭṭārakar Esevai
traikāllyayōgaṁ tamaga marepādadayda
vumeraḍuṁ sōbhisa mūṟuṁ guyuṁ
nērvvaḍe samitigaḷayduṁ samaṁ nindi rārāji
sepatraṁ viṁśadguṇapadapudidoḍaṁvī tisā
rtthadoḷ poḻdudintappesakaṁ traiga rmmi sandarttapā
damahimeyiṁ śrī vimukta vratīndra [r .] mahi
yantuṁ bhāga saye varaṁ brahmacaryya
vrataṁ baṁdisuṁ siddhāntadēva . nā saṁmaṁdhigakalācatura
traividya prasabhaṁ vyāvṛtta nmatigādhā
ṟindā dēvasthānaṅga ḷavanivaḷa
kali mātsaryya muṁṭe . niyamā cāravicārasā
raguṇabhārajñāna saṁsāranirṇnaya nirvyaktiniyō
ganirmmāṇacitra māryyā yayiṁtā
ḻdidati venisutalī Aṇni
yabhaṭṭārakarannavallamuniparvvirśvambharābhā
doḷ . saṁtata parṇnavatsaḷavaṁśōnnatiyoḷ
jinavaktranirggatapātrakaḷāprapūrṇna marṁme
yiṁ sujanōdbhaṭa riṣṭa
viṁśvadbhava rindu candraraṁ prabhāca
ndramunīndaraṁ pogaḷutirppadu vāgvida
rī bhūtaḷaṁ . sakaḷavyākaraṇaṁ nighaṁṭu
gaṇitaṁ vātsāyanaṁ jyōtiśaṁ śakunaṁ chanda
manuvu gāṁdhara . maḷaṁkāraṁ mahākā vyanā
ṭaka madhyātmikamartthaśāstra tati siddhānta pra
māṇaṅgaḷeṁba kaḷāvidye ganantavīryyamu
nipa rssarvvajñarī dhātriyoḷ .
Eḍeyuṁ maṭe masēvyamakuṭaṁ yi brahmāṇḍa
nahipanepēḷe yi bīḷa nīḷduṁ pāṁ
kīrttidēvamunipatiya
parama
mapariṣa
va nāśraTwo or three more

ratnamakuṭaṁ cūḍāma
ṇi samastadānadharmmā
nurāgapriye satyabra
tayukte mōdavidyādha
ri sattiganaṇuṁgi rūpavi
ḷāse yatyudārasāḷiṇi
guṇaratnanidhāneyara
ppa śri manmahādēviya
rāḷva maravoḷala basadi
ge śrīmajjagadēkama
lladēvara pādepadmōpa
jīvigaḷappa ghaṭeya
ṁkakāṟa noḷaṁba(bha)rasa
noḷaṁbavāḍiyu kariviḍi mūva
ttanāḷuttamire sakava
rṣa 946 . raktākṣi saṁva
tsarada srāhiyoḷ U
ttarāyaṇasaṁkrāmaṇa
dandu śrīmatguṇakīrtti
siddhāntabhaṭṭārakara si
ṣya dēvakīrttipaṇḍita
rgge pāvarise
vāḷeppa
ḷage pa
llitu
yi mattari The continuation is lost. mutilated. ōnly the word bhaṭṭiraka is seen in the 26th line.
Digital edition of SII 11.61 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
